intro: Gymnopteris aliena, also known as the alien strap fern, is a species of fern native to the tropical regions of the world, characterized by its unique strap-like leaves and delicate fronds. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Gymnopteris aliena include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas and infusions, as well as topical applications, such as salves and creams, which are used to treat skin conditions and promote wound healing.
